turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough that just won't go away, it's been two weeks now, and I'm also experiencing some difficulty breathing and wheezing, especially when I exert myself. It's really affecting my daily life and work as a teacher, so I'm hoping we can figure out what's going on and get me feeling better soon.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: I've been feeling really overwhelmed by this cough. It started out as a dry, annoying sensation, but then it changed and now I'm producing yellowish-green sputum. It's getting worse over time, even when I'm not doing anything strenuous.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some low-grade fever, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius, and it tends to get worse in the evenings. Sometimes I feel really tired and fatigued.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Yes, actually... I've been feeling quite hot and sweaty when my symptoms hit, especially at night. It's really uncomfortable and makes me feel even more exhausted in the mornings.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: I tend to get really hot and sweat a lot when my symptoms are at their worst.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: I do get some chest pain, but it's on the right side and feels sharp.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: No, the pain is mostly confined to my right side, and it gets worse when I take deep breaths or cough.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience any numbness or tingling in my arms. It's just a sharp pain on one side that makes me uncomfortable.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, nothing like that. Just this persistent cough and difficulty breathing that's really getting in the way of my daily life.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling or unusual sensations in my extremities.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: No, nothing like that. But to be honest, I have been feeling a bit fatigued and run down lately, which is making it hard for me to keep up with my daily activities.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: I've actually been experiencing some decreased appetite over the past few days, which is unusual for me. I just haven't felt like eating much lately.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: No, I don't really feel queasy or nauseous.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: I've had a low-grade fever, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius, and it tends to get worse in the evenings.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: I haven't really felt like eating much lately, which is unusual for me. And I have been feeling a bit more tired than usual, even when I'm not exerting myself.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient:
As an elementary school teacher, my days are pretty busy and unpredictable. I try to eat healthy most of the time, but with a crazy schedule, it's easy to grab something quick on the go or skip meals when I'm short on time. I do try to get some exercise by taking long walks during my breaks, but lately, my energy levels have been really low so I've had to scale back on that too.

I also work closely with young children every day, which can be fun and rewarding, but it's also a high-exposure job for illnesses. Several of my students have been out sick in the past month, which has been concerning for me.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I try to stay relatively active, but lately, I've had to scale back on my exercise routine due to fatigue.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: My appetite has really been off lately. I try to stick to simple meals like oatmeal or toast for breakfast, and sometimes I'll have a salad or soup for lunch if I can muster up the energy.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma that she manages well with medication, and my grandmother on her side passed away from lung cancer when I was younger.
